                 By default, <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> property
                 and are not included in the JSON schema. If the parameter is optional, such that a default value is provided,
                 <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> is allowed to be <see langword="null"/>; otherwise, <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/>
                 must be non-<see langword="null"/>, or else the invocation will fail with an exception due to the required nature of the parameter.
                 The handling of <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters can be overridden via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/>.
               </item>
               <item>
                 By default, <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> parameters are bound directly to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> instance
                 passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and are not included in the JSON schema. If the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>
                 instance passed to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> is <see langword="null"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> implementation
                 manufactures an empty instance, such that parameters of type <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> can always be satisfied, whether
                 optional or not. The handling of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> parameters can be overridden via
                 <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/>.
               </item>
             </list>
             All other parameter types are, by default, bound from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             and are included in the generated JSON schema. This can be overridden by the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/> provided
             via the <paramref name="options"/> parameter; for every parameter, the delegate is enabled to choose if the parameter should be included in the
             generated schema and how its value should be bound, including handling of optionality (by default, required parameters that are not included in the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary will result in an exception being thrown). Loosely-typed additional context information can be passed
             into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> via the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Context"/> dictionary; the default
             binding ignores this collection, but a custom binding supplied via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/> can choose to
             source arguments from this data.
             </para>
             <para>
             The default marshaling of parameters from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary permits values to be passed into the <paramref name="method"/>'s
             invocation directly if the object is already of a compatible type. Otherwise, if the argument is a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>, <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonDocument"/>,
             or <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonNode"/>, it is deserialized into the parameter type, utilizing <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> if provided,
             or else using <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>. If the argument is anything else, it is round-tripped through JSON, serializing the object as JSON
             and then deserializing it to the expected type.
             </para>
             <para>
             In general, the data supplied via an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary is supplied from an AI service and should be considered
             unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of <paramref name="method"/>, consider having <paramref name="method"/>
             point to an instance method on an instance configured to hold the appropriate state. An <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameter can also be
             used to resolve services from a dependency injection container.
             </para>
             <para>
             By default, return values are serialized to <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> using <paramref name="options"/>'s
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> if provided, or else using <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>.
             However, return values whose declared type is <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, a derived type of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, or
             any type assignable from <see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> (e.g. <c>AIContent[]</c>, <c>List<AIContent></c>) are
             special-cased and are not serialized: the created function returns the original instance(s) directly to enable
             callers (such as an <c>IChatClient</c>) to perform type tests and implement specialized handling. If
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> is supplied, that delegate governs the behavior instead.
             Handling of return values can be overridden via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/>.
             </para>
             </remarks>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="method"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <exception cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonException">A parameter to <paramref name="method"/> is not serializable.</exception>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory.Create(System.Delegate,System.String,System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
             <summary>Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance for a method, specified via a delegate.</summary>
             <param name="method">The method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</param>
             <param name="name">
             The name to use for the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>. If <see langword="null"/>, the name will be derived from
             any <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DisplayNameAttribute"/> on <paramref name="method"/>, if available, or else from the name of <paramref name="method"/>.
             </param>
             <param name="description">
             The description to use for the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>. If <see langword="null"/>, a description will be derived from
             any <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute"/> on <paramref name="method"/>, if available.
             </param>
             <param name="serializerOptions">The <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> used to marshal function parameters and any return value.</param>
             <returns>The created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             Any parameters to <paramref name="method"/> are sourced from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary
             of key/value pairs and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>. There are a few exceptions to this:
             <list type="bullet">
               <item>
                 <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to the <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> passed into
                 the invocation via <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>'s <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameter. The parameter is
                 not included in the generated JSON schema.
               </item>
               <item>
                 By default, <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> property
                 and are not included in the JSON schema. If the parameter is optional, such that a default value is provided,
                 <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> is allowed to be <see langword="null"/>; otherwise, <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/>
                 must be non-<see langword="null"/>, or else the invocation will fail with an exception due to the required nature of the parameter.
               </item>
               <item>
                 By default, <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> parameters are bound directly to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> instance
                 passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and are not included in the JSON schema. If the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>
                 instance passed to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> is <see langword="null"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> implementation
                 manufactures an empty instance, such that parameters of type <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> can always be satisfied, whether
                 optional or not.
               </item>
             </list>
             All other parameter types are bound from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             and are included in the generated JSON schema.

             <summary>
             Adds to the agent pipeline an anonymous delegating agent based on a delegate that provides
             an implementation for both <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
             </summary>
             <param name="sharedFunc">
             A delegate that provides the implementation for both <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. This delegate is invoked with the list of messages, the agent
             session, the run options, a delegate that represents invoking the inner agent, and a cancellation token. The delegate should be passed
             whatever messages, session, options, and cancellation token should be passed along to the next stage in the pipeline.
             It will handle both the non-streaming and streaming cases.
             </param>
             <returns>The updated <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder"/> instance.</returns>
             <remarks>
             This overload can be used when the anonymous implementation needs to provide pre-processing and/or post-processing, but doesn't
             need to interact with the results of the operation, which will come from the inner agent.

             <summary>
             Adds to the agent pipeline an anonymous delegating agent based on a delegate that provides
             an implementation for both <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
             </summary>
             <param name="runFunc">
             A delegate that provides the implementation for <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. When <see langword="null"/>,
             <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> must be non-null, and the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             will use <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> for the implementation.
             </param>
             <param name="runStreamingFunc">
             A delegate that provides the implementation for <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. When <see langword="null"/>,
             <paramref name="runFunc"/> must be non-null, and the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             will use <paramref name="runFunc"/> for the implementation.
             </param>
             <returns>The updated <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder"/> instance.</returns>
             <remarks>
             One or both delegates can be provided. If both are provided, they will be used for their respective methods:
             <paramref name="runFunc"/> will provide the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>, and
             <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> will provide the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
             If only one of the delegates is provided, it will be used for both methods. That means that if <paramref name="runFunc"/>
             is supplied without <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/>, the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             will employ limited streaming, as it will be operating on the batch output produced by <paramref name="runFunc"/>. And if
             <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> is supplied without <paramref name="runFunc"/>, the implementation of
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> will be implemented by combining the updates from <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/>.
             </remarks>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException">Both <paramref name="runFunc"/> and <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> are <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder.UseAIContextProviders(Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageAIContextProvider[])">
             <summary>
             Adds one or more <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageAIContextProvider"/> instances to the agent pipeline, enabling message enrichment
             for any <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>.
             </summary>
             <param name="providers">
             The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageAIContextProvider"/> instances to invoke before and after each agent invocation.
             Providers are called in sequence, with each receiving the output of the previous provider.
             </param>
             <returns>The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder"/> with the providers added, enabling method chaining.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="providers"/> is empty.</exception>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             This method wraps the inner agent with a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.DelegatingAIAgent"/> that calls each provider's
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageAIContextProvider.InvokingAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageAIContextProvider.InvokingContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> in sequence before the inner agent runs,
             and calls <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIContextProvider.InvokedAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIContextProvider.InvokedContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> on each provider after the inner agent completes.
             </para>
             <para>
             This allows any <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> to benefit from <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageAIContextProvider"/>-based
             context enrichment, not just agents that natively support <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIContextProvider"/> instances.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all the information:

## Summary: Microsoft.Agents.AI 1.3.0 & Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI 10.3.0 API Surface

Based on exploration of the NuGet packages, here's the complete API surface for building an agentic console app:

### 1. **Namespaces**

**Microsoft.Agents.AI** (Main Agent Framework):
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI` - Core agent classes, ChatClientAgent, options, sessions, builders
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions` - Base abstractions (AIAgent, AgentRunOptions, AgentSession, etc.)
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI.Compaction` - Message history compaction strategies

**Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (Core AI abstractions - in separate package):
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` - IChatClient, AIFunction, AITool, ChatOptions, ChatMessage, etc.
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions` - Abstract base classes

**Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI** (OpenAI integration):
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` - Extension methods on OpenAI clients

---

### 2. **Creating a ChatClientAgent**

**Constructor Overview** (from `Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent`):

```csharp
// Primary constructor with full options
public ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    ChatClientAgentOptions options,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? services = null)

// Simplified constructor
public ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    string? instructions,
    string? name,
    string? description,
    IList<AITool> tools,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? services = null)
```

**Key Properties of ChatClientAgentOptions**:
- `Instructions` - System instructions for agent behavior
- `Name` - Agent identifier
- `Description` - Human-readable description
- `Tools` - List of AITool instances (functions the agent can call)

---

### 3. **Defining Tools (AIFunction/AITool)**

**Tool Definition using AIFunctionFactory** (from `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory`):

```csharp
// Simple method-based tool creation
AIFunction tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(MyFunction);

[Description("Gets the weather for a location")]
static string GetWeather(string location) => "Sunny";

// With custom options
AIFunction tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    MyFunction,
    name: "custom_name",
    description: "Custom description",
    serializerOptions: options);

// From delegate
AIFunction tool = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    (AIFunctionArguments args) => MyMethod(args["param"]));
```

**AITool Structure** (what gets passed to agent):
- `Name` - Tool identifier
- `Description` - Describes what tool does (used by LLM to decide to call it)
- `AdditionalProperties` - Any extra metadata

**AIFunction inheritance**:
- Derives from `AIFunctionDeclaration`
- Has `InvokeAsync(AIFunctionArguments, CancellationToken)` method
- Has `JsonSchema` property describing parameters

---

### 4. **Invoking the Agent**

**Main invocation methods on AIAgent** (base class in abstractions):

```csharp
// No message (uses session context/instructions)
Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(
    AgentSession session,
    AgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

// With string message
Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(
    string message,
    AgentSession session,
    AgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

// With single ChatMessage
Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(
    ChatMessage message,
    AgentSession session,
    AgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

// With multiple messages (core method)
Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(
    IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages,
    AgentSession session,
    AgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

// Streaming versions
IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(
    AgentSession session,
    AgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

// Similar overloads for streaming...
```

**ChatClientAgent-specific invocation**:

```csharp
// ChatClientAgent adds these methods
Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(
    AgentSession session,
    ChatClientAgentRunOptions options,  // Specialized options
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(
    AgentSession session,
    ChatClientAgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)
```

---

### 5. **AgentRunOptions & ChatClientAgentRunOptions**

**Base AgentRunOptions** properties (in abstractions):
- `ContinuationToken` - For resuming background responses
- `AllowBackgroundResponses` - Enable async/polling responses
- `AdditionalProperties` - Custom properties dict
- `ResponseFormat` - ChatResponseFormat for structured output

**ChatClientAgentRunOptions** (extends AgentRunOptions):
```csharp
public class ChatClientAgentRunOptions : AgentRunOptions
{
    public ChatOptions ChatOptions { get; set; }  // Chat-specific options
    public Func<IChatClient, IChatClient> ChatClientFactory { get; set; }  // Decorator factory
}
```

**ChatOptions** contains:
- `Temperature`, `TopP`, `TopK` - Sampling parameters
- `MaxOutputTokens` - Response length limit
- `Tools` - List of AITool instances
- `ToolMode` - Auto/Required/None
- `AllowMultipleToolCalls` - Multiple functions per response
- `Instructions` - Per-request instructions
- `ConversationId` - For stateful clients
- `FrequencyPenalty`, `PresencePenalty` - Token penalties
- `Seed` - For reproducibility
- `StopSequences` - Stop tokens
- `ResponseFormat` - Structured output format

---

### 6. **Tool Registration with Agent**

**At ChatClientAgent creation time**:

```csharp
// Method 1: Via options
var options = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Instructions = "You are a helpful assistant",
    Name = "MyAgent",
    Tools = new List<AITool>
    {
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather),
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetTime)
    }
};
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, options);

// Method 2: Via simplified constructor
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    instructions: "You are helpful",
    name: "MyAgent",
    description: "Does things",
    tools: new[] {
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather),
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetTime)
    });

// Method 3: Per-request via ChatClientAgentRunOptions
var runOptions = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions
{
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new[] { AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather) }
    }
};
await agent.RunAsync("What's the weather?", session, runOptions);
```

---

### 7. **Creating OpenAI Chat Client via MEAI**

**Extension methods on OpenAI ChatClient** (from `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAIClientExtensions`):

```csharp
// Primary method
IChatClient client = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

// Alternative with Responses client
IChatClient client = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponsesClient(apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

// With Assistants API
IChatClient client = new OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantClient(apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient(assistantId: "asst_...", threadId: "thread_...");

// With decorator pattern
IChatClient baseClient = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

IChatClient decorated = new ChatClientBuilder(baseClient)
    .UseFunctionInvocation()      // Enable tool calling
    .UseDistributedCache(cache)   // Add caching
    .UseOpenTelemetry()           // Add tracing
    .Build();
```

---

### 8. **Complete Minimal Example Pattern**

```csharp
using Microsoft.Agents.AI;
using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;

// 1. Create IChatClient from OpenAI
var chatClient = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

// 2. Create tools
[System.ComponentModel.Description("Gets the current weather")]
static string GetWeather(string location) => "Sunny, 72°F";

var tools = new[] {
    AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather)
};

// 3. Create agent options
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Instructions = "You are a helpful assistant",
    Name = "WeatherBot",
    Tools = tools
};

// 4. Create agent
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, agentOptions);

// 5. Create session
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync();

// 6. Invoke agent
var runOptions = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions();
var response = await agent.RunAsync(
    "What's the weather in NYC?",
    session,
    runOptions);

Console.WriteLine(response.Message.Content);
```

---

### 9. **Key Return Types**

**AgentResponse** (non-streaming):
- `Message` - ChatMessage from agent
- `ContinuationToken` - For background responses

**AgentResponseUpdate** (streaming):
- `StreamingUpdate` - Incremental content
- `ContinuationToken` - For resuming

**AgentSession**:
- Maintains conversation state
- Can be serialized/deserialized to JSON
- Created via `agent.CreateSessionAsync()`

---

### 10. **Building Agent Pipelines (Optional)**

```csharp
// Chain agents together
var builder = new AIAgentBuilder(baseAgent);
builder
    .Use(agent => new DecoratorAgent(agent))  // Add middleware
    .UseAIContextProviders(provider1, provider2);  // Add context

var pipeline = builder.Build();
```

This covers the complete API surface for building an agentic console app with tools using these packages!

Look in the NuGet cache at ~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.3.0/ for the DLL and any XML docs.

Specifically find:
1. What namespace(s) are used (Microsoft.Agents.AI.*)
2. How to create a ChatClientAgent or similar agent class
3. How to define tools (AIFunction, or similar)
4. How to invoke the agent (InvokeAsync, InvokeStreamingAsync)
5. What AgentInvokeOptions looks like (MaximumIterations, etc.)
6. How tools are registered with the agent

Also explore Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI (version 10.3.0) at ~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/10.3.0/ to understand:
1. How to create an OpenAI chat client via MEAI
2. Extension methods like AsIChatClient or AddOpenAIChatClient

Look at XML doc files, decompile if needed, or check for README files in the packages. Use `dotnet tool install -g ilspy` or just examine the XML docs and any .md files.

```csharp
Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(
    AgentSession session,
```
```csharp
    ChatClientAgentRunOptions options,  // Specialized options
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)

IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(
    AgentSession session,
    ChatClientAgentRunOptions options,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken)
```

---

### 5. **AgentRunOptions & ChatClientAgentRunOptions**

**Base AgentRunOptions** properties (in abstractions):
- `ContinuationToken` - For resuming background responses
- `AllowBackgroundResponses` - Enable async/polling responses
- `AdditionalProperties` - Custom properties dict
- `ResponseFormat` - ChatResponseFormat for structured output

**ChatClientAgentRunOptions** (extends AgentRunOptions):
```csharp
public class ChatClientAgentRunOptions : AgentRunOptions
{
    public ChatOptions ChatOptions { get; set; }  // Chat-specific options
    public Func<IChatClient, IChatClient> ChatClientFactory { get; set; }  // Decorator factory
}
```

**ChatOptions** contains:
- `Temperature`, `TopP`, `TopK` - Sampling parameters
- `MaxOutputTokens` - Response length limit
- `Tools` - List of AITool instances
- `ToolMode` - Auto/Required/None
- `AllowMultipleToolCalls` - Multiple functions per response
- `Instructions` - Per-request instructions
- `ConversationId` - For stateful clients
- `FrequencyPenalty`, `PresencePenalty` - Token penalties
- `Seed` - For reproducibility
- `StopSequences` - Stop tokens
- `ResponseFormat` - Structured output format

---

### 6. **Tool Registration with Agent**

**At ChatClientAgent creation time**:

```csharp
// Method 1: Via options
var options = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Instructions = "You are a helpful assistant",
    Name = "MyAgent",
    Tools = new List<AITool>
    {
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather),
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetTime)
    }
};
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, options);

// Method 2: Via simplified constructor
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    instructions: "You are helpful",
    name: "MyAgent",
    description: "Does things",
    tools: new[] {
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather),
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetTime)
    });

// Method 3: Per-request via ChatClientAgentRunOptions
var runOptions = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions
{
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions
    {
        Tools = new[] { AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather) }
    }
};
await agent.RunAsync("What's the weather?", session, runOptions);
```

---

### 7. **Creating OpenAI Chat Client via MEAI**

**Extension methods on OpenAI ChatClient** (from `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAIClientExtensions`):

```csharp
// Primary method
IChatClient client = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

// Alternative with Responses client
IChatClient client = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponsesClient(apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

// With Assistants API
IChatClient client = new OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantClient(apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient(assistantId: "asst_...", threadId: "thread_...");

// With decorator pattern
IChatClient baseClient = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

IChatClient decorated = new ChatClientBuilder(baseClient)
    .UseFunctionInvocation()      // Enable tool calling
    .UseDistributedCache(cache)   // Add caching
    .UseOpenTelemetry()           // Add tracing
    .Build();
```

---

### 8. **Complete Minimal Example Pattern**

```csharp
using Microsoft.Agents.AI;
using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;

// 1. Create IChatClient from OpenAI
var chatClient = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey)
    .AsIChatClient();

// 2. Create tools
[System.ComponentModel.Description("Gets the current weather")]
static string GetWeather(string location) => "Sunny, 72°F";

var tools = new[] {
    AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather)
};

// 3. Create agent options
var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
{
    Instructions = "You are a helpful assistant",
    Name = "WeatherBot",
    Tools = tools
};

// 4. Create agent
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, agentOptions);

// 5. Create session
var session = await agent.CreateSessionAsync();

// 6. Invoke agent
var runOptions = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions();
var response = await agent.RunAsync(
    "What's the weather in NYC?",
    session,
    runOptions);

Console.WriteLine(response.Message.Content);
```

---

### 9. **Key Return Types**

**AgentResponse** (non-streaming):
- `Message` - ChatMessage from agent
- `ContinuationToken` - For background responses

**AgentResponseUpdate** (streaming):
- `StreamingUpdate` - Incremental content
- `ContinuationToken` - For resuming

**AgentSession**:
- Maintains conversation state
- Can be serialized/deserialized to JSON
- Created via `agent.CreateSessionAsync()`

---

### 10. **Building Agent Pipelines (Optional)**

```csharp
// Chain agents together
var builder = new AIAgentBuilder(baseAgent);
builder
    .Use(agent => new DecoratorAgent(agent))  // Add middleware
    .UseAIContextProviders(provider1, provider2);  // Add context

var pipeline = builder.Build();
```

This covers the complete API surface for building an agentic console app with tools using these packages!

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
<member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions">
            <summary>
            Represents metadata for a chat client agent, including its identifier, name, instructions, and description.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            This class is used to encapsulate information about a chat client agent, such as its unique
            identifier, display name, operational instructions, and a descriptive summary. It can be used to store and transfer
            agent-related metadata within a chat application.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Id">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the agent id.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Name">
--
            Creates a new instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions"/> with the same values as this instance.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentRunOptions">
            <summary>
            Provides specialized run options for <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent"/> instances, extending the base agent run options with chat-specific configuration.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            This class extends <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> to provide additional configuration options that are specific to
            chat client agents, in particular <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentRunOptions.ChatOptions"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentRunOptions.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentRunOptions"/> class.
            </summary>
=== AgentResponseUpdate ===
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder.Use(System.Func{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse}},System.Func{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate}})">
            <summary>
            Adds to the agent pipeline an anonymous delegating agent based on a delegate that provides
            an implementation for both <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
            </summary>
            <param name="runFunc">
            A delegate that provides the implementation for <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. When <see langword="null"/>,
            <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> must be non-null, and the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            will use <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> for the implementation.
            </param>
            <param name="runStreamingFunc">
--
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AnonymousDelegatingAIAgent.#ctor(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Func{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse}},System.Func{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate}})">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AnonymousDelegatingAIAgent"/> class.
            </summary>
            <param name="innerAgent">The inner agent.</param>
            <param name="runFunc">
            A delegate that provides the implementation for <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AnonymousDelegatingAIAgent.RunCoreAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. When <see langword="null"/>,
            <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> must be non-null, and the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AnonymousDelegatingAIAgent.RunCoreAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            will use <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> for the implementation.
            </param>
            <param name="runStreamingFunc">
--
            <returns>An asynchronous enumerable of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> instances representing the streaming response.</returns>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.String,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
            <summary>

<member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions">
            <summary>
            Represents metadata for a chat client agent, including its identifier, name, instructions, and description.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            This class is used to encapsulate information about a chat client agent, such as its unique
            identifier, display name, operational instructions, and a descriptive summary. It can be used to store and transfer
            agent-related metadata within a chat application.

            an implementation for both <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
            </summary>
            <param name="runFunc">
            A delegate that provides the implementation for <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. When <see langword="null"/>,
            <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> must be non-null, and the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            will use <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> for the implementation.
            </param>
            <param name="runStreamingFunc">
            A delegate that provides the implementation for <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. When <see langword="null"/>,
            <paramref name="runFunc"/> must be non-null, and the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            will use <paramref name="runFunc"/> for the implementation.
            </param>
            <returns>The updated <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentBuilder"/> instance.</returns>
            <remarks>
            One or both delegates can be provided. If both are provided, they will be used for their respective methods:
            <paramref name="runFunc"/> will provide the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>, and
            <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> will provide the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
            If only one of the delegates is provided, it will be used for both methods. That means that if <paramref name="runFunc"/>
            is supplied without <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/>, the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            will employ limited streaming, as it will be operating on the batch output produced by <paramref name="runFunc"/>. And if
            <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/> is supplied without <paramref name="runFunc"/>, the implementation of
            <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> will be implemented by combining the updates from <paramref name="runStreamingFunc"/>.
            </remarks>

grep -A3 'ChatClient' ~/.nuget/packages/openai/2.10.0/lib/net8.0/OpenAI.xml 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'param name.*model\|param name.*key\|param name.*cred' | head -10

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check response types and OpenAI constructors

data.reasoningText:
Now I can see the actual API:

1. `ChatClientAgentOptions` has: `Id`, `Name`, `Description`, `ChatOptions`, `ChatHistoryProvider`, `AIContextProviders`, `UseProvidedChatClientAsIs`, etc. - but NOT `Instructions` or `Tools` directly. The instructions and tools go via `ChatOptions`.

2. The simplified constructor accepts `instructions`, `name`, `description`, `tools` as individual parameters.

3. `AgentResponseUpdate` - I need to check what properties it has.

4. For `ApiKeyCredential`, it seems the OpenAI package uses `System.ClientModel.ApiKeyCredential`.
   
I need to address a few things: use `System.ClientModel.ApiKeyCredential` or pass the API key string directly to the OpenAI ChatClient, switch to the simplified ChatClientAgent constructor with individual parameters instead of ChatClientAgentOptions, and verify the streaming property on AgentResponseUpdate.

data.content: Now I have the full API. Let me fix the build errors:
data.reasoningText:
Now I have the full API:

1. `ChatClientAgent` simplified constructor: `(IChatClient chatClient, string? instructions, string? name, string? description, IList<AITool> tools, ...)`
2. `ChatClientAgentOptions` has `ChatOptions` (not `Instructions` or `Tools` directly — those go via ChatOptions or through the simplified constructor)
3. `AgentResponseUpdate` has `Text` property (not `StreamingUpdate`)
4. OpenAI `ChatClient` takes `(string model, string apiKey)` directly — no need for `ApiKeyCredential`

Let me also check: `ChatClientAgentRunOptions` constructor takes `ChatOptions` as param.


Now I'm updating Program.cs to match the actual API.

 };
 
-// --- Create agent ---
-var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
-{
-    Name = "ResearchAgent",
-    Instructions = """
-        You are a thorough research agent. Your goal is to research a topic by:
-        
-        1. SEARCH: Use SearchWeb to find relevant information about the topic.
-           Run multiple searches with different queries to get comprehensive coverage.
-        
-        2. READ: Use FetchPage to read promising web pages from the search results.
-        
-        3. TAKE NOTES: Use AddNote to save key findings as you go.
-           Each note should capture one distinct fact or insight with its source.
-        
-        4. REVIEW: Use GetNotes to review all collected notes before summarizing.
-        
-        5. SUMMARIZE: After gathering enough information (at least 3-5 notes),
-           write a well-structured summary of your findings. Include:

+// --- Create agent with instructions and tools ---
+const string instructions = """
+    You are a thorough research agent. Your goal is to research a topic by:
+    
+    1. SEARCH: Use SearchWeb to find relevant information about the topic.
+       Run multiple searches with different queries to get comprehensive coverage.
+    
+    2. READ: Use FetchPage to read promising web pages from the search results.
+    
+    3. TAKE NOTES: Use AddNote to save key findings as you go.
+       Each note should capture one distinct fact or insight with its source.
+    
+    4. REVIEW: Use GetNotes to review all collected notes before summarizing.
+    
+    5. SUMMARIZE: After gathering enough information (at least 3-5 notes),
+       write a well-structured summary of your findings. Include:
+       - An overview of the topic
+       - Key facts and details
+       - Different perspectives if applicable
+       - Sources for the information
+    
+    Be systematic: search, read, note, then summarize. Do not fabricate information.
+    Only include facts you found through your tools.
+    """;
+
+var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
+    chatClient,
+    instructions: instructions,
+    name: "ResearchAgent",
+    description: "An agent that researches topics by searching the web and taking notes",
+    tools: tools);

data.content:
The .NET 10 research agent is built and compiles cleanly. Here's what it does:

**Architecture** (per skill guidance: `Microsoft.Agents.AI` + MEAI for agentic tool-calling):

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Program.cs` | Wires up `IChatClient` → `ChatClientAgent` with tools, streams the response |
| `Tools/WebSearchTool.cs` | `SearchWeb` (DuckDuckGo API, no key needed) + `FetchPage` (reads URLs) |
| `Tools/NoteTool.cs` | `AddNote`, `GetNotes`, `DeleteNote` — in-memory research notebook |

**Agent workflow**: The agent searches → reads pages → takes notes → reviews notes → writes a summary with sources.

**To run**:
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="your-key-here"
dotnet run --project ResearchAgent
```

Optionally set `OPENAI_MODEL` to override the default `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`.
